title: alisma californicum
intro: Alisma californicum, also known as California water plantain or western water plantain, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the Alismataceae family, typically found in wetlands and shallow waters throughout the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve symptoms of urinary tract infections and edema. The health benefits of Alisma californicum include its ability to support kidney function, reduce bloating and swelling, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Alisma californicum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and can also be consumed as a tea to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
